key_insight: HistoSeg++ improves biomarker segmentation by combining Nested-UNet multiscale feature fusion with attention and channel recalibration to better capture context and boundaries.
clinical_relevance: More accurate biomarker segmentation can support downstream biomedical image analysis and potentially improve the consistency of pathology-focused research workflows.
method: Nested-UNet backbone for multiscale contextual feature capture
Inner and outer attention units to refine upsampling focus
Squeeze-and-excitation modules plus edge-aware loss to boost channel weighting and boundary accuracy
evidence_signal: The method was tested on three publicly available benchmark datasets and showed better generalization than existing Nested-UNet methods.
watch_item: Results are promising, but performance depends on dataset quality and the model may still require careful validation before use across new imaging settings.
paper_title: HistoSeg++: Delving deeper with attention and multiscale feature fusion for biomarker segmentation
